title: betula pubescens var. nigricans
intro: Betula pubescens var. nigricans, also known as black birch, is a variety of the birch tree that is native to North America and is characterized by its dark, glossy bark and sweet, wintergreen-flavored inner bark. The medicinal properties of black birch include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of black birch include relieving pain and reducing inflammation, as well as treating digestive issues and colds. Black birch is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, salves, and infusions, and can be consumed as a wintergreen-flavored tea or used topically to relieve muscle and joint pain.
